mappings.rationaleAI: Computing technology covering architecture, operating systems, web servers, and network communication addresses computers (D), types of computers (D.1), applications (D.4), information processing (H), electronic networks (I.8), and integrated circuits (K.1.c).
Algorithms, programming, computational complexity, and the Turing machine address computers (D), programming systems (D.2), computer languages (D.3), and AI as a development in computing (D.5).

History from counting aids through mechanical calculation to electronic computation covers calculating devices (B), computers (D), types including analogue and digital (D.1), and information processing (H).
AI covering expert systems, natural-language processing, and machine consciousness directly addresses developments in artificial intelligence (D.5).

Information theory covering measurement in bits, probability, entropy, and Turing's contributions addresses communication and information theory (A), information processing (H), elements including analog and digital forms (H.1), and the Internet as electronic network (I.8).

Collection, storage, and analysis of massive datasets addresses information processing (H), recording and storage (H.1.b), organization and retrieval (H.1.c), and public information utilities (H.2.b).
Cryptography covering symmetric-key and public-key systems, identity verification, and post-quantum algorithms directly addresses encryption and decryption techniques and devices (I.9).
Network theory covering topology, connectivity, and analysis of communications technology addresses communication theory (A), electronic networks (I.8), and electronic components (K).
Photography from daguerreotype origins through digital-era imaging directly addresses the technology of photography (G) and still photography (G.1).
Journalism encompassing newspapers, broadcasting, television, radio, and digital media addresses publishing (I.1), radio communications (I.5), and television communications (I.6).
Robotics covering autonomous machines and their relationship to AI connects to developments in artificial intelligence including robotics (D.5).
Sound recording, playback, broadcasting, and streaming technology addresses sound recording and reproducing devices (F) including mechanical (F.1), magnetic (F.2), and optical systems (F.3).
Lasers and optical technologies connect to active components (K.1.a) and passive components including waveguides (K.1.b) in communications electronics.
Wave physics covering electromagnetic radiation and its applications connects to communication theory (A), radio communications (I.5), radar systems (J.1), and sonar systems (J.2).
Electronic sound production and reproduction alongside acoustic principles address sound recording and reproducing devices (F).
Surveillance systems using data collection and digital technologies connect to detection and remote sensing systems (J) and electronic networks (I.8).
Cinema technology covering sound, colour, and digital transitions addresses recording and reproducing devices (F) and motion-picture photography (G.2).

Typography covering letterform development, typeface design, and printing from early printing through digital media addresses printing machines and processes (E.3) and production of printing plates (E.4).
Legal frameworks governing creative works including copyright in digital contexts connect to information processing and systems (H) in the context of knowledge dissemination.
Applications in silicon chips, liquid crystal displays, and magnetic memory directly address semiconductor devices (K.1.a) and integrated circuits (K.1.c).
History and cultural significance of dictionaries including online dictionaries directly addresses dictionaries and lexicons as educational and reference systems (H.2.c.i).
Nanoscale science including quantum confinement and molecular machinery connects to integrated circuits and miniaturized electronic components (K.1.c).
Individual identification through measurement of personal attributes including facial features and fingerprints connects to detection and remote sensing (J) and sensing devices and transducers (K.2).
Covers practical applications of number theory in internet cryptography and data encryption, directly relevant to encryption and decryption techniques and devices.
Covers mass surveillance, data protection law, and digital communications challenges, relevant to electronic networks and encryption/decryption techniques.
Covers navigational instruments including radio and electronic equipment, relevant to radio communications systems and radar/remote sensing systems.
